summary:
Apple Original Films' "F1 The Movie," directed by Joseph Kosinski, will be available for streaming on Apple TV starting December 12, 2025. The film, starring Brad Pitt, has already achieved remarkable success in theaters, becoming the highest-grossing sports film of all time with over $629 million in global box office revenue. It also holds the title of the highest-grossing original feature this year and marks Brad Pitt's most successful film to date. The movie has received an A CinemaScore and a 97% audience score on Rotten Tomatoes, celebrated for its authenticity as the most realistic racing feature ever made.

The film follows Sonny Hayes (Brad Pitt), a former Formula 1 prodigy who nearly saw his career end due to a track accident. Decades later, he's approached by his former teammate Ruben (Javier Bardem) to help save a struggling Formula 1 team. Alongside rookie Joshua Pearce (Damson Idris), Sonny faces both internal and external challenges, highlighting that in Formula 1, your teammate can be your fiercest competitor. The film was shot during actual Grand Prix weekends, adding to its authenticity.

Beyond its box office success, "F1 The Movie" has also made a significant cultural impact through its soundtrack, "F1® The Album," which has sold over 650,000 albums and garnered over a billion streams globally. The album features Tate McRae's hit "Just Keep Watching," which alone has amassed over four billion views on short-form video platforms, making it a defining cultural moment of 2025.
